fields.summary: Umbrella JIRA of SLS fixes / improvements

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.
status.name: Open

issuetype.description: A problem which impairs or prevents the functions of the product.
issuetype.name: Bug

fields.description: Right now, nodes are added sequentially and it can take a long time if there are large number of nodes. With this change nodes will be added in parallel and thus reduce the node addition time.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

This message was automatically generated.

Thanks [~abmodi] for the patch.

Few comments:

1) You have this instruction {{int threadPoolSize = Math.max(poolSize, 10);}} instead of 10 you should have {{SLSConfiguration.RUNNER_POOL_SIZE_DEFAULT;}}

{{2)executorService.awaitTermination(1, TimeUnit.HOURS);}} I think the awaiting time is too long. How did you come up with this value?

This message was automatically generated.
[~elgoiri] Could you please review and commit it. Thanks

[~giovanni.fumarola] do you have further comments?
Do existing unit test cover that the number of generated nodes is correct?

[~elgoiri] Yes existing tests cover number of generated nodes is correct. There is a check for waitForNodesRunning which will stuck infinitely if number of running nodes doesn't match number of generated nodes.

Thanks [~abmodi] for the explanation.

LGTM +1.

[~abmodi], the patch does not apply to trunk cleanly anymore; I guess it was the one we pushed a couple days ago.
Do you mind rebasing?

Committed to trunk.

SUCCESS: Integrated in Jenkins build Hadoop-trunk-Commit #14035 (See [https://builds.apache.org/job/Hadoop-trunk-Commit/14035/])
YARN-8137. Parallelize node addition in SLS. Contributed by Abhishek (inigoiri: rev fd24fd0ff771a6cba3097179fbb040d3b4f2a2d1)
* (edit) hadoop-tools/hadoop-sls/src/main/java/org/apache/hadoop/yarn/sls/SLSRunner.java
Thanks [~elgoiri] and [~giovanni.fumarola].
